article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions — July 28, 2026
body:
Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions - July 28, 2026
Agenda
Agenda
Open Video in new Window

COUNCIL CORRESPONDENCE AND PUBLIC NOTICE SUBMISSIONS
Tuesday,
July 28, 2026
at
12
:00
P
.
m
.
-
12
:00
P
.
m

1.
SECTION 1 - NOTE FROM STAFF
Correspondence that required a note from staff. Legislative Services will respond to the writers listed in this section providing the information noted or forthcoming.
1.1
Queenie Kwan Yi Yip - Traffic Related Concerns in the Brentwood Area
1.
Queenie Yip - Concerns Regarding Road Closures.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, Deputy General Manager Finance, General Manager Engineering
Note from General Manager Engineering
: The recent closures of Dawson Street and Alpha Avenue were reviewed and approved through the City’s traffic permitting process, where staff trying to mitigate traffic impacts while still facilitating development projects within the Brentwood Town Centre area. The City requires the developer and their contractors to inform the public of their activities appropriately.  City staff will continue to monitor and work closely with developers to ensure work is carried out in consideration with the larger neighbourhood. Staff will respond back to the resident regarding their inquiries related to transportation details and enhancements.
2.
SECTION 2 -  REFERRED TO ADVISORY BODIES
Correspondence referred to Advisory Bodies. Legislative Services will respond to the writers listed in this section advising of the referral.
2.1
Executive Committee of Council - September 8, 2026
2.1.1
Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association - Grant Request
1.
Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association Grant Request.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Grant Request Letter - Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association.pdf
3.
Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association Grant Request - Appendix A.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, Acting GM Corporate Services, Acting Chief Financial Officer
Note from Legislative Services
:
Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area was dissolved in April,2026. It is noted that while the underlying management entity, Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association, was and is registered as a formal non-profit society, they are not able to act as an official BIA, levy property taxes, or represent the City.
2.2
Transportation Committee - September 24, 2026

3.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to consider traffic concerns in Brentwood area
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report from Queenie Kwan Yi Yip about traffic-related concerns in the Brentwood area. This is a proposed discussion — no decisions have been made yet. The report may lead to future bylaw changes or traffic measures that could affect local businesses and residents.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 1.1 Queenie Kwan Yi Yip - Traffic Related Concerns in the Brentwood Area
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road BIA grant request up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a grant request from the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA). If approved, this grant would provide funding to support local businesses in the North Road area. The decision is not yet final — council will vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact your Burnaby council member to express support or concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.1.1 Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association - Grant Request
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to address bus-bike lane conflict on 145 route
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal from HUB Cycling Burnaby to address a conflict zone on the 145 bus route where buses and bikes share the same lane. The proposal aims to improve safety for both cyclists and transit users. A decision has not yet been made.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.2.1 HUB Cycling Burnaby - Bus and Bike Lane Conflict Zone on 145 Route
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Regional micro-mobility share network under consideration
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to move towards a regional micro-mobility share network. This would involve shared bikes, e-bikes, and scooters that could be used across multiple cities in the region. The proposal is still in early stages and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 2.2.2 Markus Lau - Moving Towards a Regional Micro-Mobility Share Network
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver board decisions for June 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report from the Metro Vancouver board meeting held on June 26, 2026. The report outlines proposed decisions and actions from the regional board that could affect businesses in Burnaby and across the region. A proposed bylaw or policy change may be up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.1 Metro Vancouver - Board in Brief - June 26, 2026
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to respond to BC housing targets progress report
agenda_briefs.summary: The Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs acknowledged Burnaby's six-month housing targets progress report, noting the city reported 910 net-new units (59% of its Year 1 target of 1,536 units) and has 35,961 instream units at various approval stages. The city adopted amendments to its Official Community Plan and zoning bylaws, including a transition from Floor Area Ratio to a Height-Based Development Framework. The first annual report is due on or before December 15, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby first annual housing targets report due

agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.2 Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs - Response to Submission of the Six-Month Housing Targets Progress Report

attachments.title: Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs - Response to Submission of the Six-Mo

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Merritt bylaw update on releasing pigeons
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider an update to the City of Merritt's bylaw regarding the releasing of pigeons. A proposed bylaw would set new rules for when and how pigeons can be released within city limits. This is still a proposal and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.3 City of Merritt - Bylaw Update - Releasing of Pigeons
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Central Okanagan climate action program funding continuation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider continuing funding for the Local Government Climate Action Program in the Regional District of Central Okanagan. This proposed decision would keep money flowing to local climate initiatives, which could affect businesses through future energy or emissions rules.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.4 Regional District of Central Okanagan - Local Government Climate Action Program Funding Continuation

attachments.title: Regional District of Central Okanagan - Local Government Climate Action Program

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Lake Cowichan 'Elect Respect' campaign under consideration
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed 'Elect Respect' campaign from the Town of Lake Cowichan. This is a proposal that has not yet been approved or enacted. The campaign would aim to promote respectful conduct during elections.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.5 Town of Lake Cowichan - Elect Respect
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider aircraft noise complaints over North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on aircraft noise complaints from North Burnaby residents. The report may lead to proposed actions or requests to address the issue, but no decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider stronger heat protections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a call from the Canadian Association of Physicians for the Environment to adopt a bylaw requiring landlords and property owners to keep at least one living space per rental unit at or below 26°C, following New Westminster's model. The proposal also includes establishing 24-hour public cooling centres during extreme heat events and incentivizing electric heat pump installations. A written response outlining the municipality's plan is requested.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could impose a 26°C maximum temperature requirement on rental properties.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.7 Canadian Association of Physicians for the Environment - Call for Stronger Heat Protections following the Fifth Anniversary of the BC Heat Dome

attachments.title: Canadian Association of Physicians for the Environment - Call for Stronger Heat

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — July 28, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to review Burnaby's 90-night short-term rental limit
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Burnaby resident Hani Golestaneh to review the 90-night annual cap on short-term rentals under Bylaw No. 14272. The request asks Council to evaluate whether the cap is still necessary given existing principal-residence and licensing requirements, benchmark Burnaby's rules against other Metro Vancouver municipalities, and provide data justifying the original 28/90 thresholds. No decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your short-term rental operations in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 3.8 Hani Golestaneh - Request to Review the 90-Night Short-Term Rental Limit (Bylaw No. 14272)

attachments.title: Hani Golestaneh - Request to Review the 90-Night Short-Term Rental Limit (Bylaw
